experiment: F-CITY1 adjacency routing — neighborhood spillover in UCB1
hypothesis: Domain adjacency routing improves dispatch outcomes. Domains adjacent to high-scoring domains get a UCB1 boost, creating agglomeration economics.
method: Added adjacency bonus to dispatch_scoring.py ucb1_score(). Loads adjacency graph from DOMAIN.md Adjacent: headers. Top-10 scoring domains identified, their neighbors get +0.2 per high-scoring neighbor (capped at +0.6).

pre_registration.expect: Adjacency bonus activates for ≥8 domains. Bonus range 0.2-0.6. Does not distort top-3 ranking but lifts well-connected peripheral domains.
pre_registration.falsification: No reward difference between adjacent and non-adjacent DOMEX lanes after 20 lanes.

ranking_impact.note: expert-swarm rose from #3 (6.7) to #3 (7.3) — adjacency reinforced but did not distort. Peripheral domains (evolution, finance, information-science) lifted toward mid-pack.

code_location: tools/dispatch_scoring.py:ucb1_score() — adjacency bonus block after soul multiplier
status: WIRED — tracking reward difference over next 20 DOMEX lanes
next: Measure whether adjacent-domain DOMEX lanes produce higher reward (merge rate, lesson yield) than non-adjacent after 20 lanes. Also: seed more DOMAIN.md Adjacent: headers (currently 16/52).
